messages.content: email to staff regarding a TS's Behaviour Support Plan Training with Sydney Gecsh from Positive Moods on Tuesday, 21st November at Hope Church, Kids Ministy Room from 3pm to 5pm. Please let me know if you are available to attend.

Dear staff,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to inform you about an upcoming training session on Tuesday, 21st November. The training will focus on TS's Behaviour Support Plan and will be conducted by Sydney Gecsh from Positive Moods.

The session will take place at Hope Church, specifically in the Kids Ministry Room, from 3pm to 5pm. It is important that as many staff members as possible attend this training, as it will greatly benefit our understanding of TS's behaviours and how to effectively support them.

If you are available to attend, please let me know by responding to this email. Your presence and active participation would greatly contribute to the success of this training session. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
her plan is avaailable on Airtable, please review her plan before the training session

Dear staff,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to remind you about the upcoming training session on Tuesday, 21st November regarding TS's Behaviour Support Plan. This session will be facilitated by Sydney Gecsh from Positive Moods.

Before attending the session, it is highly recommended that you review TS's Behaviour Support Plan, which is accessible on Airtable. This will allow you to familiarize yourself with the plan and come prepared with any questions or clarifications you may have.

If you encounter any issues accessing the plan on Airtable or have any questions, please reach out to me or the designated person in charge of the plan.

Thank you for your cooperation and preparation for the training session. Your active participation will contribute greatly to its success.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
the lock box at Yodelay house was found open today and the keys are missing. Does anyone know where they are?

Dear staff,

I hope you are all doing well. I wanted to bring to your attention a concerning situation that occurred today at Yodelay House. The lock box at the facility was found open, and unfortunately, the keys are now missing.

It is crucial that we locate these keys as soon as possible to ensure the security of the premises. If anyone has any information regarding the whereabouts of the keys or noticed anything suspicious, please inform me immediately.

In the meantime, we advise everyone to be extra vigilant and report any unusual or suspicious activity to the appropriate authorities. We will also be taking additional security measures to ensure the safety of Yodelay House and its occupants.

Thank you for your attention to this matter, and please reach out to me if you have any information regarding the missing keys.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
email subject
URGENT: Missing Keys for Lock Box at Yodelay House
Email to staff welcoming a new member to our Bethany Team, Ann Swanson.

Subject: Welcoming Ann Swanson to Our Bethany Team!

Dear team,

I hope this email finds you all well. I am delighted to announce that we have a new member joining our Bethany team - Ann Swanson!

Ann will be starting with us on [start date] as a [position]. She brings with her [briefly mention relevant experience or qualifications]. We are excited to have her onboard and believe that her skills and expertise will greatly contribute to the success of our team and the organization as a whole.

I kindly request all of you to extend a warm welcome to Ann and make her feel comfortable in her new role. As the newest member, she will undoubtedly have a lot of questions and will appreciate your support and guidance during her initial days.

Please take a moment to introduce yourselves to Ann and offer any assistance or insight that can help her transition smoothly into her position. Collaboration and teamwork are pillars of our organization, and by extending a helping hand to Ann, we demonstrate our commitment to building a strong and supportive team dynamic.

Once again, let's join together in welcoming Ann to our Bethany team. We look forward to working with her and achieving new heights of success!

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Email to staff welcoming a new member to our Bethany Team, Ann Swanson. formally an educator with 3 years experience as an aged care worker.

Subject: Warm Welcome to Ann Swanson - Our Newest Bethany Team Member!

Dear team,

I am thrilled to share some exciting news with all of you. Please join me in warmly welcoming Ann Swanson to our Bethany team! Ann will be starting as a valued member of our organization on [start date] in the role of [position].

Ann brings with her a wealth of experience and expertise in the field. Previously, she worked as an educator for [number of years] and has also served as an aged care worker. Her background in these roles will undoubtedly bring a unique perspective and valuable insights to our team.

As we all know, joining a new organization can sometimes feel overwhelming. It is our collective responsibility to help Ann settle in comfortably and provide the support she needs to quickly become a productive member of our team. I encourage each and every one of you to reach out to Ann, introduce yourselves, and offer any assistance or guidance she may require during her initial period with us.

With her diverse background and skill set, Ann's contributions will surely enhance our team's effectiveness and help us deliver exceptional service to our clients. Let's embrace her arrival as an opportunity to learn from one another, grow together, and strengthen our already remarkable team spirit.

Once again, a warm welcome to Ann Swanson! We are excited to have her on board and look forward to the positive impact she will make at Bethany.

If you have any questions or need further information, please do not hesitate to reach out to me or our HR department.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

Ann brings with her a wealth of experience and expertise in the field. Previously, she worked as an educator before changing careers to serve as an aged care support worker. Her background in these roles will undoubtedly bring a unique perspective and valuable insights to our team.

Subject: Giving a Warm Welcome to Ann Swanson - Our Newest Bethany Team Member!

Dear team,

I hope this email finds you well. I am thrilled to announce that we have a fantastic addition to our Bethany team - Ann Swanson! With her diverse background and extensive experience, Ann brings a wealth of expertise in the field that will undoubtedly benefit our team and organization.

Having previously worked as an educator before embarking on a rewarding career as an aged care support worker, Ann brings a unique perspective and valuable insights to our team. Her knowledge and varied experiences will contribute to the holistic care we provide to our clients. We are grateful to have her join us, and I am confident that her presence will greatly enrich our team dynamic.

I invite each and every one of you to extend a warm and heartfelt welcome to Ann. As she settles into her new role, let's ensure she feels supported, valued, and encouraged. Please take a moment to introduce yourselves, share your areas of expertise, and offer any assistance or guidance she may need during her initial days.

We value collaboration and teamwork at Bethany, and Ann's arrival is an opportunity for us to strengthen our bond and collectively strive for excellence. Together, we will continue to make a difference in the lives of our clients and create positive change within our community.

Please join me in extending a warm welcome to Ann Swanson as she embarks on this exciting journey with us. Let's work together to ensure she feels right at home in our Bethany family.

If you have any questions or need further information, please do not hesitate to reach out to me or our HR department.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
email to Sydney to confirm TS's Behaviour Support Plan training session will be on Tuesday, 21st November at Hope Church, building 4/175 Varsity Parade, Varsity Lakes at 3pm to 5pm.

Subject: Confirmation - TS's Behaviour Support Plan Training Session

Dear Sydney,

I hope this email finds you well. I wanted to confirm the details for TS's Behaviour Support Plan training session that we discussed earlier.

The training session will indeed be on Tuesday, 21st November at the following location:

Hope Church, Building 4/175 Varsity Parade,
Varsity Lakes.

The session will take place from 3pm to 5pm in the designated Kids Ministry Room.

I appreciate your willingness to conduct this training and share your expertise with our staff. We believe that your guidance will greatly benefit our understanding of TS's behaviours and help us provide effective support.

If there are any changes or adjustments to the schedule or location, please let me know as soon as possible. We want to ensure that all logistics are well-arranged for a successful training session.

Thank you once again for your dedication and commitment to this training. We look forward to meeting you and gaining valuable insights from your expertise.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
welcome Ann Swanson to our team photo caption

"Join us in giving a warm welcome to Ann Swanson, our newest team member! We are excited to have her expertise and unique perspective on board as we work together to create positive change. Welcome, Ann!"
try again
"Introducing Ann Swanson, the newest addition to our team! Let's give her a warm welcome as we embark on this exciting journey together. We're thrilled to have you, Ann!"

Reply to this email
The drier rules -   @Teressa
you perhaps could enforce the "only for emergency" rule over there too.  Same as you did at Maidenhair.
Some washing was done Tuesday night (and I know it was not the night for washing for CP but he missed doing his bedding and towels on Monday, he said. Which is the new care plan/task list)    Splitting up some of his washing as he used to do it all on one day and it was far too much to cope with for the clothes line. (clothes, bedding, bath mats, towels - could be 4 or 5 loads of washing)
CP - went to put his 1st load straight in the drier. I said, what are you doing that for? it is not raining. You need to hang it out. He said, it's gong to rain. I encouraged him to hang this load out, which he did.
In the morning CP - did one load of clothes washing on HOT wash. I asked, what for? he said the towels, etc. were smelly.  Personally, I never wash in the HOT cycle.  Should anyone be washing on the HOT cycle?
So perhaps a sign could be put on the drier, and the residents also over there could all be told the same thing, that the drier shoud be for "emergencies" and rainy weather only.  
The dishwasher - I have seen that be put on the Pots and Pans cycle, for a smallish load as well, when it probably only needs regular or a Quick cycle.  CP often - just choose cycles for clothes or dishes that are the longest, as he thinks everything needs it.

Dear [Sender],

Thank you for bringing up the concerns regarding the use of the dryer and dishwasher. I understand the need to enforce the "only for emergencies" rule, similar to what we have implemented at Maidenhair.

It's important for everyone to be aware of the appropriate use of these appliances. I will take immediate action to put up a sign on the dryer reminding residents that it should be reserved for emergencies and rainy weather only. Additionally, I will communicate this information to all residents at the facility to ensure everyone is on the same page.

Regarding the dishwasher, I will remind all residents that it's important to choose the appropriate cycle for the load being washed. Opting for the shortest and most efficient cycle, such as the regular or quick cycle, is generally recommended for smaller loads. I will make sure to emphasize this to all residents to help maximize the efficiency of our dishwasher usage.

Thank you for bringing these matters to my attention. Your feedback is greatly appreciated as we strive to create a comfortable and efficient living environment for all residents. If you have any further concerns or suggestions, please do not hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

We understand that CP's behaviour of concerns can be at times difficult to manage. His mental health is his disability, and he needs constant reminding, guidance and reassurance. Please be set firm boundaries and routines. continue to record his behavious of concerns and eating habits and when Obert returns we will arrange a house meeting to discuss any issues as a team.

Dear [Sender],

Thank you for addressing your concerns regarding CP's behavior and the need for firm boundaries and routines. We understand that managing his mental health can be challenging and that he requires constant reminders, guidance, and reassurance.

Rest assured that we are committed to providing CP with the necessary support and care. We will continue to monitor and record his behaviors of concern, as well as his eating habits, to better understand his needs and make appropriate adjustments to his care plan.

Once Obert returns, we will prioritize organizing a house meeting to discuss any issues as a team. This will provide an opportunity for open communication, sharing insights, and collectively brainstorming effective solutions. The well-being and comfort of all residents are of utmost importance to us, and we will strive to address any concerns or challenges collaboratively.

If you have any other suggestions or specific strategies that have proven successful in managing CP's behavior, please feel free to share them with us. We value your input and expertise in ensuring the best possible care for all residents.

Thank you for your understanding and support. We will continue to work together as a team to provide the highest level of care for CP and all residents in our facility.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
